steps.title: Graph the lines and look for parallel distinct graphs
steps.content: Each choice compares the same left side, $-13x-\frac{1}{9}$, to a possible right side. A no-solution equation happens when the two graphs are parallel and distinct: same slope, different intercept.

steps.title: Identify the no-solution choice
steps.content:
Choice B (‘$-13x-\frac{1}{9}=-13x+\frac{1}{9}$’) is
$$-13x-\frac{1}{9}=-13x+\frac{1}{9}$$
Both sides have slope $-13$, but their intercepts are different. The lines never meet, so there is no solution.

steps.title: Check the others quickly
steps.content: Choice A (‘$-13x-\frac{1}{9}=-13x-\frac{1}{9}$’) has identical sides, so it has infinitely many solutions. The other choices have slope $13$ on the right side, so each intersects the left-side line once. Therefore only B has no solution.
